---
title: "Mathematical and Search Substrates"
chapter_id: "mathematical-and-search-substrates"
part_id: "stack-layers"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "coilmoecot"
  - "temporal_coil_research"
  - "cognitive_compilation"
  - "genesiscode"
  - "viea"
evidence_level: "argument"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# Mathematical and Search Substrates

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `mathematical-and-search-substrates` |
| Part | Part II - The Layers of the ASI Stack |
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `coilmoecot`, `temporal_coil_research`, `cognitive_compilation`, `genesiscode`, `viea` |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from `book_structure.json` and the source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

Exploratory representations may improve search, routing, or compression, but the book needs to keep these hypotheses falsifiable.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

Novel mathematical substrates can become overclaimed if they are not tied to tests, baselines, and failure criteria.

## Core claim

[mathematical-and-search-substrates.core, support: argument] Alternative substrates belong in the stack only as testable implementation hypotheses until evidence supports them.

## Mechanism

- Frame coil, calculus, and geometric representations as optional specialist substrates.
- Define what they would need to improve.
- Specify falsification tests before adoption into core architecture.

## Interfaces

- Routing can treat a substrate as a specialist core.
- Compression can test representation efficiency.
- Evidence layers compare against baselines.

## Invariants

- Exploratory claims stay exploratory.
- Performance claims require measured baselines.
- Failed hypotheses remain recorded.

## Failure modes

- Overclaiming performance.
- Using opaque math as authority.
- Adopting a substrate without regression tests.

## Minimal implementation

A technical appendix and experiment plan with baseline comparisons and adoption gates.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Baseline search comparison | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Representation efficiency test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| GPU parallelization feasibility test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Failure-case and falsification review | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `coilmoecot` | CoilMoECOT Whitepaper v2.0 | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Prime-temporal coil networks as governed specialist cores in MoECOT runtime. |
| `temporal_coil_research` | Temporal Coil Research | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Manifest-driven A/B process for temporal-coil integration in language model training. |
| `cognitive_compilation` | Cognitive Compilation | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Compiler framing for LLM-centered planning, semantic IR, target compilation, incremental repair. |
| `genesiscode` | GenesisCode | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Tiny pure calculus + obligations + provenance for auditable AI-symbiotic programming. |
| `viea` | Verified Intent-to-Execution Architecture | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Keystone source. Human intent -> command contracts -> artifacts -> routing -> runtime targets -> verification -> deployment -> feedback. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
